title: Lothiania mordax
intro: Lothiania mordax, a plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, is a species of flowering plant within the family Menispermaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lothiania mordax include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lothiania mordax include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
